NKJV.1: But concerning the times and the seasons, brethren, you have no need that I should write to you.
NKJV.2: For you yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so comes as a thief in the night.
NKJV.3: For when they say, “Peace and safety!” then sudden destruction comes upon them, as labor pains upon a pregnant woman. And they shall not escape.
NKJV.4: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, so that this Day should overtake you as a thief.
NKJV.5: You are all sons of light and sons of the day. We are not of the night nor of darkness.
NKJV.6: Therefore let us not sleep, as others do, but let us watch and be sober.
NKJV.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk are drunk at night.
NKJV.8: But let us who are of the day be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet the hope of salvation.
NKJV.9: For God did not appoint us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
NKJV.10: who died for us, that whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with Him.
NKJV.11: Therefore comfort each other and edify one another, just as you also are doing.
NKJV.12: And we urge you, brethren, to recognize those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord and admonish you,
NKJV.13: and to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
NKJV.14: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn those who are unruly, comfort the fainthearted, uphold the weak, be patient with all.
NKJV.15: See that no one renders evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue what is good both for yourselves and for all.
NKJV.18: in everything give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
NKJV.21: Test all things; hold fast what is good.
NKJV.22: Abstain from every form of evil.
NKJV.23: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you completely; and may your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.24: He who calls you is faithful, who also will do it.
NKJV.26: Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
NKJV.27: I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read to all the holy brethren.
NKJV.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

ESV.1: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers,  you have no need to have anything written to you.
ESV.2: For you yourselves are fully aware that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
ESV.3: While people are saying, “There is peace and security,” then sudden destruction will come upon them as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
ESV.4: But you are not in darkness, brothers, for that day to surprise you like a thief.
ESV.5: For you are all children  of light, children of the day. We are not of the night or of the darkness.
ESV.6: So then let us not sleep, as others do, but let us keep awake and be sober.
ESV.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, are drunk at night.
ESV.8: But since we belong to the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.
ESV.9: For God has not destined us for wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
ESV.10: who died for us so that whether we are awake or asleep we might live with him.
ESV.11: Therefore encourage one another and build one another up, just as you are doing.
ESV.12: We ask you, brothers, to respect those who labor among you and are over you in the Lord and admonish you,
ESV.13: and to esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
ESV.14: And we urge you, brothers, admonish the idle,  encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with them all.
ESV.15: See that no one repays anyone evil for evil, but always seek to do good to one another and to everyone.
ESV.18: give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
ESV.21: but test everything; hold fast what is good.
ESV.22: Abstain from every form of evil.
ESV.23: Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.24: He who calls you is faithful; he will surely do it.
ESV.26: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
ESV.27: I put you under oath before the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers.
ESV.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

NASB.1: Now as to the times and the epochs, brethren, you have no need of anything to be written to you.
NASB.2: For you yourselves know full well that the day of the Lord will come just like a thief in the night.
NASB.3: While they are saying, 'Peace and safety!' then destruction will come upon them suddenly like labor pains upon a woman with child, and they will not escape.
NASB.4: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, that the day would overtake you like a thief;
NASB.5: for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We are not of night nor of darkness;
NASB.6: so then let us not sleep as others do, but let us be alert and sober.
NASB.7: For those who sleep do their sleeping at night, and those who get drunk get drunk at night.
NASB.8: But since we are of [the] day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet, the hope of salvation.
NASB.9: For God has not destined us for wrath, but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
NASB.10: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with Him.
NASB.11: Therefore encourage one another and build up one another, just as you also are doing.
NASB.12: But we request of you, brethren, that you appreciate those who diligently labor among you, and have charge over you in the Lord and give you instruction,
NASB.13: and that you esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Live in peace with one another.
NASB.14: We urge you, brethren, admonish the unruly, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with everyone.
NASB.15: See that no one repays another with evil for evil, but always seek after that which is good for one another and for all people.
NASB.18: in everything give thanks; for this is God's will for you in Christ Jesus.
NASB.21: But examine everything [carefully]; hold fast to that which is good;
NASB.22: abstain from every form of evil.
NASB.23: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you entirely; and may your spirit and soul and body be preserved complete, without blame at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.24: Faithful is He who calls you, and He also will bring it to pass.
NASB.26: Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
NASB.27: I adjure you by the Lord to have this letter read to all the brethren.
NASB.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

NLT.1: Now concerning how and when all this will happen, dear brothers and sisters, we don’t really need to write you.
NLT.2: For you know quite well that the day of the Lord’s return will come unexpectedly, like a thief in the night.
NLT.3: When people are saying, “Everything is peaceful and secure,” then disaster will fall on them as suddenly as a pregnant woman’s labor pains begin. And there will be no escape.
NLT.4: But you aren’t in the dark about these things, dear brothers and sisters, and you won’t be surprised when the day of the Lord comes like a thief.
NLT.5: For you are all children of the light and of the day; we don’t belong to darkness and night.
NLT.6: So be on your guard, not asleep like the others. Stay alert and be clearheaded.
NLT.7: Night is the time when people sleep and drinkers get drunk.
NLT.8: But let us who live in the light be clearheaded, protected by the armor of faith and love, and wearing as our helmet the confidence of our salvation.
NLT.9: For God chose to save us through our Lord Jesus Christ, not to pour out his anger on us.
NLT.10: Christ died for us so that, whether we are dead or alive when he returns, we can live with him forever.
NLT.11: So encourage each other and build each other up, just as you are already doing.
NLT.12: Dear brothers and sisters, honor those who are your leaders in the Lord’s work. They work hard among you and give you spiritual guidance.
NLT.13: Show them great respect and wholehearted love because of their work. And live peacefully with each other.
NLT.14: Brothers and sisters, we urge you to warn those who are lazy. Encourage those who are timid. Take tender care of those who are weak. Be patient with everyone.
NLT.15: See that no one pays back evil for evil, but always try to do good to each other and to all people.
NLT.18: Be thankful in all circumstances, for this is God’s will for you who belong to Christ Jesus.
NLT.19: Do not stifle the Holy Spirit.
NLT.21: but test everything that is said. Hold on to what is good.
NLT.22: Stay away from every kind of evil.
NLT.23: Now may the God of peace make you holy in every way, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless until our Lord Jesus Christ comes again.
NLT.24: God will make this happen, for he who calls you is faithful.
NLT.25: Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us.
NLT.26: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a sacred kiss.
NLT.27: I command you in the name of the Lord to read this letter to all the brothers and sisters.
NLT.28: May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

WEB.1: But concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need that anything be written to you.
WEB.2: For you yourselves know well that the day of the Lord comes like a thief in the night.
WEB.3: For when they are saying, “Peace and safety,” then sudden destruction will come on them, like birth pains on a pregnant woman; and they will in no way escape.
WEB.4: But you, brothers, aren’t in darkness, that the day should overtake you like a thief.
WEB.5: You are all children of light, and children of the day. We don’t belong to the night, nor to darkness,
WEB.6: so then let’s not sleep, as the rest do, but let’s watch and be sober.
WEB.7: For those who sleep, sleep in the night; and those who are drunk are drunk in the night.
WEB.8: But let us, since we belong to the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and, for a helmet, the hope of salvation.
WEB.9: For God didn’t appoint us to wrath, but to the obtaining of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
WEB.10: who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.
WEB.11: Therefore exhort one another, and build each other up, even as you also do.
WEB.12: But we beg you, brothers, to know those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you,
WEB.13: and to respect and honor them in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
WEB.14: We exhort you, brothers, admonish the disorderly, encourage the faint-hearted, support the weak, be patient toward all.
WEB.15: See that no one returns evil for evil to anyone, but always follow after that which is good, for one another, and for all.
WEB.18: In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you.
WEB.21: Test all things, and hold firmly that which is good.
WEB.22: Abstain from every form of evil.
WEB.23: May the God of peace himself sanctify you completely. May your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.24: He who calls you is faithful, who will also do it.
WEB.26: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
WEB.27: I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the holy brothers.
WEB.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

CSB17.1: About the times and the seasons: <sup>ⓖ</sup> Brothers and sisters, you do not need anything to be written to you.
CSB17.2: For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord <sup>ⓗ</sup> will come just like a thief <sup>ⓘ</sup> in the night.
CSB17.3: When they say, “Peace and security,” then sudden destruction <sup>ⓙ</sup> will come upon them, like labor pains on a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
CSB17.4: But you, brothers and sisters, are not in the dark, for this day to surprise you like a thief. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.5: For you are all children of light and children of the day. We do not belong to the night or the darkness. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.6: So then, let us not sleep, like the rest, but let us stay awake and be self-controlled.
CSB17.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, get drunk at night.
CSB17.8: But since we belong to the day, let us be self-controlled <sup>ⓜ</sup> and put on the armor <sup>ⓝ</sup> of faith and love, and a helmet <sup>ⓐ</sup> of the hope of salvation.
CSB17.9: For God did not appoint us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord <sup>ⓑ</sup> Jesus Christ, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.10: who died for us, <sup>ⓓ</sup> so that whether we are awake or asleep, <sup>ⓔ</sup> we may live together with him. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.11: Therefore encourage one another and build each other up as you are already doing.
CSB17.12: Now we ask you, brothers and sisters, to give recognition <sup>ⓖ</sup> to those who labor among you and lead you <sup>[1]</sup> in the Lord and admonish you,
CSB17.13: and to regard them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace <sup>ⓗ</sup> among yourselves.
CSB17.14: And we exhort you, brothers and sisters: <sup>ⓘ</sup> warn those who are idle, <sup>[2]</sup> comfort the discouraged, help the weak, be patient <sup>ⓙ</sup> with everyone.
CSB17.15: See to it that no one repays evil for evil <sup>ⓚ</sup> to anyone, <sup>ⓛ</sup> but always pursue what is good for one another and for all. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.18: give thanks in everything; for this is God’s will <sup>ⓐ</sup> for you in Christ Jesus.
CSB17.21: but test all things. <sup>ⓑ</sup> Hold on to what is good.
CSB17.22: Stay away <sup>ⓒ</sup> from every kind of evil.
CSB17.23: Now may the God of peace <sup>ⓓ</sup> himself sanctify you completely. And may your whole spirit, <sup>ⓔ</sup> soul, and body be kept sound and blameless at the coming <sup>ⓕ</sup> of our Lord <sup>ⓖ</sup> Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.24: He who calls <sup>ⓘ</sup> you <sup>ⓙ</sup> is faithful; he will do it.
CSB17.25: Brothers and sisters, pray for us also.
CSB17.26: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
CSB17.27: I charge you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the brothers and sisters. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

AMP.1: Now as to the times and dates, brothers and sisters, you have no need for anything to be written to you.
AMP.2: For you yourselves know perfectly well that the day of the [return of the] Lord is coming just as a thief [comes unexpectedly and suddenly] in the night.
AMP.3: While they are saying, "Peace and safety [all is well and secure!]" then [in a moment unforeseen] destruction will come upon them suddenly like labor pains on a woman with child, and they will absolutely not escape [for there will be no way to escape the judgment of the Lord].
AMP.4: But you, believers, [all you who believe in Christ as Savior and acknowledge Him as God's Son] are not in spiritual darkness [nor held by its power], that the day [of judgment] would overtake you [by surprise] like a thief;
AMP.5: for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We do not belong to the night nor to darkness.
AMP.6: So then let us not sleep [in spiritual indifference] as the rest [of the world does], but let us keep wide awake [alert and cautious] and let us be sober [self-controlled, calm, and wise].
AMP.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who are drunk get drunk at night.
AMP.8: But since we [believers] belong to the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet, the hope and confident assurance of salvation.
AMP.9: For God has not destined us to [incur His] wrath [that is, He did not select us to condemn us], but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
AMP.10: who died [willingly] for us, so that whether we are awake (alive) or asleep (dead) [at Christ's appearing], we will live together with Him [sharing eternal life].
AMP.11: Therefore encourage and comfort one another and build up one another, just as you are doing.
AMP.12: Now we ask you, brothers and sisters, to appreciate those who diligently work among you [recognize, acknowledge, and respect your leaders], who are in charge over you in the Lord and who give you instruction,
AMP.13: and [we ask that you appreciate them and] hold them in the highest esteem in love because of their work [on your behalf]. Live in peace with one another.
AMP.14: We [earnestly] urge you, believers, admonish those who are out of line [the undisciplined, the unruly, the disorderly], encourage the timid [who lack spiritual courage], help the [spiritually] weak, be very patient with everyone [always controlling your temper].
AMP.15: See that no one repays another with evil for evil, but always seek that which is good for one another and for all people.
AMP.16: Rejoice always and delight in your faith;
AMP.18: in every situation [no matter what the circumstances] be thankful and continually give thanks to God; for this is the will of God for you in Christ Jesus.
AMP.19: Do not quench [subdue, or be unresponsive to the working and guidance of] the [Holy] Spirit.
AMP.20: Do not scorn or reject gifts of prophecy or prophecies [spoken revelations—words of instruction or exhortation or warning].
AMP.21: But test all things carefully [so you can recognize what is good]. Hold firmly to that which is good.
AMP.22: Abstain from every form of evil [withdraw and keep away from it].
AMP.23: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you through and through [that is, separate you from profane and vulgar things, make you pure and whole and undamaged—consecrated to Him—set apart for His purpose]; and may your spirit and soul and body be kept complete and [be found] blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.24: Faithful and absolutely trustworthy is He who is calling you [to Himself for your salvation], and He will do it [He will fulfill His call by making you holy, guarding you, watching over you, and protecting you as His own].
AMP.25: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
AMP.26: Greet all the believers with a holy kiss [as brothers and sisters in God's family].
AMP.27: I solemnly charge you by the Lord to have this letter read before all the congregation.
AMP.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

MSG.1: I don't think, friends, that I need to deal with the question of when all this is going to happen.
MSG.2: You know as well as I that the day of the Master's coming can't be posted on our calendars. He won't call ahead and make an appointment any more than a burglar would.
MSG.3: About the time everybody's walking around complacently, congratulating each other—"We've sure got it made! Now we can take it easy!"—suddenly everything will fall apart. It's going to come as suddenly and inescapably as birth pangs to a pregnant woman.
MSG.4: But friends, you're not in the dark, so how could you be taken off guard by any of this?
MSG.5: You're sons of Light, daughters of Day. We live under wide open skies and know where we stand.
MSG.6: So let's not sleepwalk through life like those others. Let's keep our eyes open and be smart.
MSG.7: People sleep at night and get drunk at night.
MSG.8: But not us! Since we're creatures of Day, let's act like it. Walk out into the daylight sober, dressed up in faith, love, and the hope of salvation.
MSG.9: God didn't set us up for an angry rejection but for salvation by our Master, Jesus Christ.
MSG.10: He died for us, a death that triggered life. Whether we're awake with the living or asleep with the dead, we're alive with him!
MSG.11: So speak encouraging words to one another. Build up hope so you'll all be together in this, no one left out, no one left behind. I know you're already doing this; just keep on doing it.
MSG.12: And now, friends, we ask you to honor those leaders who work so hard for you, who have been given the responsibility of urging and guiding you along in your obedience.
MSG.13: Overwhelm them with appreciation and love! Get along among yourselves, each of you doing your part.
MSG.14: Our counsel is that you warn the freeloaders to get a move on. Gently encourage the stragglers, and reach out for the exhausted, pulling them to their feet. Be patient with each person, attentive to individual needs.
MSG.15: And be careful that when you get on each other's nerves you don't snap at each other. Look for the best in each other, and always do your best to bring it out.
MSG.18: thank God no matter what happens. This is the way God wants you who belong to Christ Jesus to live.
MSG.20: and don't stifle those who have a word from the Master.
MSG.21: On the other hand, don't be gullible. Check out everything, and keep only what's good.
MSG.22: Throw out anything tainted with evil.
MSG.23: May God himself, the God who makes everything holy and whole, make you holy and whole, put you together—spirit, soul, and body—and keep you fit for the coming of our Master, Jesus Christ.
MSG.24: The One who called you is completely dependable. If he said it, he'll do it!
MSG.25: Friends, keep up your prayers for us.
MSG.26: Greet all the Christians there with a holy embrace.
MSG.27: And make sure this letter gets read to all the brothers and sisters. Don't leave anyone out.
MSG.28: The amazing grace of Jesus Christ be with you!

NIV.1: Now, brothers, about times and dates we do not need to write to you,
NIV.2: for you know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
NIV.3: While people are saying, “Peace and safety,” destruction will come on them suddenly, as labor pains on a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
NIV.4: But you, brothers, are not in darkness so that this day should surprise you like a thief.
NIV.5: You are all sons of the light and sons of the day. We do not belong to the night or to the darkness.
NIV.6: So then, let us not be like others, who are asleep, but let us be alert and self-controlled.
NIV.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, get drunk at night.
NIV.8: But since we belong to the day, let us be self-controlled, putting on faith and love as a breastplate, and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
NIV.9: For God did not appoint us to suffer wrath but to receive salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.10: He died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we may live together with him.
NIV.11: Therefore encourage one another and build each other up, just as in fact you are doing.
NIV.12:
Final Instructions
Now we ask you, brothers, to respect those who work hard among you, who are over you in the Lord and who admonish you.
NIV.13: Hold them in the highest regard in love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
NIV.14: And we urge you, brothers, warn those who are idle, encourage the timid, help the weak, be patient with everyone.
NIV.15: Make sure that nobody pays back wrong for wrong, but always try to be kind to each other and to everyone else.
NIV.18: give thanks in all circumstances, for this is God’s will for you in Christ Jesus.
NIV.19: Do not put out the Spirit’s fire;
NIV.20: do not treat prophecies with contempt.
NIV.21: Test everything. Hold on to the good.
NIV.23: May God himself, the God of peace, sanctify you through and through. May your whole spirit, soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.24: The one who calls you is faithful and he will do it.
NIV.26: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
NIV.27: I charge you before the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers.
NIV.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

NRSVCE.1: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers and sisters, you do not need to have anything written to you.
NRSVCE.2: For you yourselves know very well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
NRSVCE.3: When they say, “There is peace and security,” then sudden destruction will come upon them, as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and there will be no escape!
NRSVCE.4: But you, beloved, are not in darkness, for that day to surprise you like a thief;
NRSVCE.5: for you are all children of light and children of the day; we are not of the night or of darkness.
NRSVCE.6: So then let us not fall asleep as others do, but let us keep awake and be sober;
NRSVCE.7: for those who sleep sleep at night, and those who are drunk get drunk at night.
NRSVCE.8: But since we belong to the day, let us be sober, and put on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.
NRSVCE.9: For God has destined us not for wrath but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
NRSVCE.10: who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep we may live with him.
NRSVCE.11: Therefore encourage one another and build up each other, as indeed you are doing.
NRSVCE.12: But we appeal to you, brothers and sisters, to respect those who labor among you, and have charge of you in the Lord and admonish you;
NRSVCE.13: esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
NRSVCE.14: And we urge you, beloved, to admonish the idlers, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with all of them.
NRSVCE.15: See that none of you repays evil for evil, but always seek to do good to one another and to all.
NRSVCE.18: give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
NRSVCE.20: Do not despise the words of prophets,
NRSVCE.21: but test everything; hold fast to what is good;
NRSVCE.22: abstain from every form of evil.
NRSVCE.23: May the God of peace himself sanctify you entirely; and may your spirit and soul and body be kept sound and blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.24: The one who calls you is faithful, and he will do this.
NRSVCE.26: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
NRSVCE.27: I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all of them.
NRSVCE.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

NET.1: Now on the topic of times and seasons, brothers and sisters, you have no need for anything to be written to you.
NET.2: For you know quite well that the day of the Lord will come in the same way as a thief in the night.
NET.3: Now when they are saying, “There is peace and security,” then sudden destruction comes on them, like labor pains on a pregnant woman, and they will surely not escape.
NET.4: But you, brothers and sisters, are not in the darkness for the day to overtake you like a thief would.
NET.5: For you all are sons of the light and sons of the day. We are not of the night nor of the darkness.
NET.6: So then we must not sleep as the rest, but must stay alert and sober.
NET.7: For those who sleep, sleep at night and those who get drunk are drunk at night.
NET.8: But since we are of the day, we must stay sober by putting on the breastplate of faith and love and as a helmet our hope for salvation.
NET.9: For God did not destine us for wrath but for gaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.10: He died for us so that whether we are alert or asleep we will come to life together with him.
NET.11: Therefore encourage one another and build up each other, just as you are in fact doing.
NET.12: Now we ask you, brothers and sisters, to acknowledge those who labor among you and preside over you in the Lord and admonish you,
NET.13: and to esteem them most highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
NET.14: And we urge you, brothers and sisters, admonish the undisciplined, comfort the discouraged, help the weak, be patient toward all.
NET.15: See that no one pays back evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue what is good for one another and for all.
NET.18: in everything give thanks. For this is God’s will for you in Christ Jesus.
NET.20: Do not treat prophecies with contempt.
NET.21: But examine all things; hold fast to what is good.
NET.22: Stay away from every form of evil.
NET.23: Now may the God of peace himself make you completely holy and may your spirit and soul and body be kept entirely blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.24: He who calls you is trustworthy, and he will in fact do this.
NET.25: Brothers and sisters, pray for us too.
NET.26: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
NET.27: I call on you solemnly in the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers and sisters.
NET.28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
